soil_types: Well-drained soil with high organic matter

pests.name: Leaf Miner
pests.impact: Adult female flies puncture leaves for feeding and egg-laying, causing white spots and wilting due to water evaporation. Larvae feed on internal leaf parts, creating thin white tunnels, which hinders photosynthesis. Wounded leaves are susceptible to secondary fungal or other pathogen infections.
pests.solution: Regular field inspection before and after planting to identify damage early. Utilize natural enemies from the Hymenoptera order (Diglyphas isaea, Hemiptersenus semiabiclavus, Opius Spp) by breeding them in low-cost wooden cages or on white polythene sheets. Use yellow sticky traps to control adult flies. Use a butterfly net in the morning when leaf miners are active to reduce adult population. Install a 5-foot high net around the field before planting. Deep plowing to destroy weeds and pupae. Remove old infected leaves and dispose of damaged leaves by burying them. Avoid selecting infected seedlings from nurseries. For chemical control, use recommended insecticides: Neem extract (640g/16L) for early damage, Azadiractin 1% EC (16ml/16L), or Abamectin 18g/L EC (9.6ml/16L) if pest population is high.

pests.name: Cutworm
pests.impact: Larvae are most active at night, cutting young plant stems at the base. In mature plants, leaf consumption is observed. Damage is more prevalent in dry weather. High larval populations in the soil during tuber development can lead to tuber damage, reducing market value.
pests.solution: Deep plowing exposes larvae and pupae to predatory birds. Good weed control and field sanitation in and around the field. Regular field inspection and hand-picking of hidden larvae when populations are low. If damage is severe, apply recommended insecticides immediately after planting: Profenophos 500g/LEC (32ML/16L) or Etofenprox 100g/LEC (24ML/16L).

diseases.name: Cercospora Leaf Spot
diseases.solution: Use clean, pathogen-free seeds. Use sprinkler irrigation. Practice crop rotation. Maintain field cleanliness and keep it free of weeds. Ensure correct planting spacing. Use recommended fungicides such as Mancozeb, Maneb, Carbendazim, Chlorothalonil.

diseases.name: Ring Spot Disease
diseases.solution: Treat seeds with hot water. Use recommended fungicides such as Mancozeb, Maneb, Carbendazim, Chlorothalonil.
